claim: Agentic coding tools that wrote 20% of code in December are now writing 80% of code
actionable: Track progress of AI-assisted coding tools in your workflows to identify where they're providing the most value and concentrate on strategic oversight.
source_title: OpenAI's Greg Brockman: Why Human Attention Is the New Bottleneck

claim: Human attention is becoming the scarcest resource in AI-augmented work environments
actionable: Design AI systems and workflows to minimize the cognitive load on humans, focusing on reducing decision approval cycles rather than automating everything.
source_title: OpenAI's Greg Brockman: Why Human Attention Is the New Bottleneck

claim: Scaling laws are empirically true without full theoretical understanding; neural networks from the 1940s apply to modern compute-heavy systems with consistent capability improvements
actionable: Trust scaling law trends for model planning but continue investing in fundamental research to understand the underlying mechanisms.
source_title: OpenAI's Greg Brockman: Why Human Attention Is the New Bottleneck

claim: Within OpenAI, the governance structure and team organization is shifting significantly due to the capabilities of agentic systems
actionable: Prepare organizational structures for flatter hierarchies and smaller core teams supported by AI agents that can handle specialized tasks.
source_title: OpenAI's Greg Brockman: Why Human Attention Is the New Bottleneck

claim: Modern AI models exceed human capability in complex software engineering tasks; GPT-5.4 outperforms most software engineers and can autonomously debug, profile, and optimize systems
actionable: Delegate complex systems engineering tasks to AI coding assistants with proper oversight; focus human engineers on architectural decisions and validation.
source_title: OpenAI's Greg Brockman: Why Human Attention Is the New Bottleneck

claim: AI models can handle complex multi-step tasks like systems optimization: reading design docs, implementing specs, running profilers, and iterating on code autonomously
actionable: Design specification documents that include success criteria and performance targets; use them as input to AI coding agents for autonomous optimization.
source_title: OpenAI's Greg Brockman: Why Human Attention Is the New Bottleneck

claim: The real bottleneck in AI-augmented work has shifted from 'can the AI do it' to 'is this what I actually want' - requiring human judgment and oversight
actionable: Build approval workflows and governance systems that enable humans to quickly validate AI outputs rather than trying to prevent AI from acting.
source_title: OpenAI's Greg Brockman: Why Human Attention Is the New Bottleneck

claim: Context is now the critical constraint for AI agents - having the right information available is more important than model capability
actionable: Focus on context engineering: ensure AI systems have access to all relevant information needed for tasks rather than expecting the model to know everything.
source_title: OpenAI's Greg Brockman: Why Human Attention Is the New Bottleneck

claim: AI agents can proactively escalate issues beyond their scope (e.g., escalating to a manager without explicit permission), which requires explicit EQ training in models
actionable: Build explicit confirmation gates into agentic workflows for high-risk actions; train models on when to escalate vs. when to auto-approve based on risk assessment.
source_title: OpenAI's Greg Brockman: Why Human Attention Is the New Bottleneck

claim: Models can be trained to distinguish high-risk actions and auto-approve low-risk ones, reducing human attention burden
actionable: Use classification-based approval systems where models flag high-risk actions for human review and automatically execute low-risk ones.
source_title: OpenAI's Greg Brockman: Why Human Attention Is the New Bottleneck
